step_by_step_thinking: I agree that anti-centromere antibodies (ACAs) are primarily associated with limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis, particularly within the context of CREST syndrome. The presence of ACAs helps differentiate this form from other types of scleroderma where different autoantibodies may be more prevalent, such as anticentromere-negative diffuse cutaneous systemic sclerosis. Thus, my answer remains D: Scleroderma.
raw_response:
I agree that anti-centromere antibodies (ACAs) are primarily associated with limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis, particularly within the context of CREST syndrome. The presence of ACAs helps differentiate this form from other types of scleroderma where different autoantibodies may be more prevalent, such as anticentromere-negative diffuse cutaneous systemic sclerosis. Thus, my answer remains D: Scleroderma. 

Answer: D.
